summary: Verified MSAL authority fix (/common → /organizations). PRIMARY assertion PASSED: after clicking [data-testid=auth-microsoft-btn], MSAL popup navigates to https://login.microsoftonline.com/organizations/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=5de6c39a-...&scope=openid%20email%20profile%20offline_access&prompt=select_account&... — /common is no longer used. This resolves the reported 'unauthorized_client: The client does not exist or is not enabled for consumers' error for personal Microsoft accounts. All auth regressions pass: 11/11 backend pytest, admin login → /chat, fresh signup → /chat with welcome toast, Google GSI iframe renders, Apple SDK loads, MSAL initializes on mount with zero JS/console errors, interaction_in_progress recovery still clears sessionStorage and opens a fresh /organizations/ popup with no error toast. NOTE: iter4 jti fix is confirmed live — register+login within the same millisecond now returns two 200s with distinct access & refresh tokens. Iter4 Facebook-button finding is RESOLVED: button no longer has disabled attribute (disabled=None) and the info toast 'Facebook Sign-In is not enabled (no client secret provided).' fires correctly on click.
action_items: USER ACTION (unchanged, external): In the Azure App Registration → Authentication → 'Supported account types', ensure the app is set to 'Accounts in any organizational directory (Multitenant)'. With authority=/organizations, personal Microsoft accounts are intentionally rejected client-side, so users must sign in with a work/school account. If you WANT to allow personal accounts, both (a) change authority to /common in AuthPage.jsx line 147 AND (b) change the Azure App Registration to 'Accounts in any org directory and personal Microsoft accounts'.

USER ACTION (unchanged from iter_3): Add https://iema-ai-platform.preview.emergentagent.com as an Authorized JavaScript Origin in Google Cloud Console; also add it as a Redirect URI (SPA) in the Azure App Registration and as a Return URL in Apple Services ID.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthPage.jsx line 147: authority='https://login.microsoftonline.com/organizations' is correct for the current Azure App configuration. The inline comment accurately documents the trade-off (no personal accounts). Good code hygiene.

AuthPage.jsx MSAL module-level msalInstance is keyed on first clientId — if oauthCfg.microsoft.client_id changes at runtime, stale instance persists. Very low priority — clientId only comes from /api/auth/oauth-config which is a one-shot fetch.

auth.py create_access_token & create_refresh_token now include jti=secrets.token_hex(8) — iter4 finding fully addressed. Verified by test_login_success_immediate_after_register asserting reg_access != login_access AND reg_refresh != login_refresh with zero sleep between calls.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_oauth_new.py (replaced 1s-sleep workaround with a real jti-uniqueness assertion; added test_login_success_immediate_after_register)
test_credentials: Admin siddharth.bose@iemlabs.com / Admin@12345; dynamic test_msal_org_<ts>@example.com / Test@12345 and test_jti_<ms>@example.com / Test@12345
seed_data_creation: One admin login + two new registrations per run (test_msal_org_<ts>@example.com, test_all_oauth_<ts>@example.com, test_jti_<ms>@example.com). Not cleaned up — negligible volume.
context_for_next_testing_agent: MSAL authority change (/common → /organizations) is live and verified by direct popup URL capture. Popup URL captured: https://login.microsoftonline.com/organizations/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=5de6c39a-bf40-4f42-88d8-f755a7797cc9&scope=openid%20email%20profile%20offline_access&prompt=select_account&response_type=code&code_challenge_method=S256&... — PKCE flow, response_mode=fragment, msal.js.browser 5.17.0. Recovery from stuck 'msal.interaction.status=interaction_in_progress' also opens a fresh /organizations/ popup. jti claim is on both access & refresh tokens. Frontend loads cleanly, no MSAL init errors, no JS pageerrors on /login. Nothing outstanding for this fix.
rca of the issue: Original bug: 'unauthorized_client: The client does not exist or is not enabled for consumers' surfaced when Microsoft's /common authority tried to route personal (consumer) Microsoft accounts through an Azure App Registration whose 'Supported account types' does not permit personal accounts. Fix (already applied in AuthPage.jsx L147): change MSAL authority from https://login.microsoftonline.com/common to https://login.microsoftonline.com/organizations. This restricts sign-in to work/school Microsoft Entra accounts only, matching the Azure app's configuration. Verified via headless popup URL capture — the popup opens on /organizations/oauth2/v2.0/authorize, not /common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize. No code changes needed by main agent.
